Asset Overview

305 E. Virginia Street is a 2017-vintage, three-story mixed-use building at the northeast corner of Virginia Street and State Highway 5 in downtown McKinney, Texas. The property is 100% leased across seven suites comprising 15,000 SF of retail and office space. Tenants include Arcade 92, The Comedy Arena (Locked Out Productions), Enclave Coworking, Plug and Play McKinney, and a general contractor office, reflecting a diversified entertainment, coworking, and professional services mix.

The building benefits from immediate proximity to a newly completed 300+ unit multifamily development directly across the intersection, the new McKinney City Hall (175,000 SF, $100M+), and TUPPS Brewery's expanded Mill District location. Free public parking sits directly behind the building. An unbuilt 3,000 SF rooftop patio or third-floor leasable expansion represents embedded upside for the right buyer.

McKinney reported $325 million in visitor spending in 2025. The Sunset Amphitheater ($300M, 20,000 capacity) and Cannon Beach are scheduled for 2027 delivery, adding further demand drivers to the downtown corridor.

Informal DSCR Scenario (Best-Guess Only)

The table below reflects a personal, back-of-envelope estimate of debt service coverage under private lending rate assumptions. These figures are illustrative only and are not a formal underwriting output, term sheet, or lender commitment of any kind.

These are rough estimates based on standard mortgage math applied to seller-stated figures. They have not been independently verified. A licensed lender, financial advisor, or CPA should be consulted before drawing any conclusions from these numbers.

Informal Observation (Not a Lender Decision)

At private lending rates of 11.50–12.00% on a 70% LTV structure, estimated debt service exceeds NOI at both rate scenarios. This property appears priced for agency or conventional bank financing (where rates would be more compatible with a 6.82% cap rate) rather than private balance sheet capital at current market rates. This is a personal observation only, not a financing decision or advice.

Items Worth Confirming (Informal Checklist)

The following items are flagged based on a casual review of the offering materials. This is not a formal due diligence checklist from a lender, attorney, or broker.

  • All 7 executed leases — 2 new tenants (Enclave Coworking, WRL GC Contractor) and the 7th suite are not yet confirmed publicly; require executed copies
  • Tenant estoppel certificates — Should be collected from all 7 tenants prior to any commitment
  • 2-year operating history — Verify seller-stated NOI of $354,890 against 2023 and 2024 actuals
  • Property tax confirmation — $68,195 per OM; confirm current bill and any pending protest
  • Insurance certificate — Confirm $29,468 annualized insurance is current and covers all suites
  • Title commitment — Confirm fee simple ownership, no undisclosed encumbrances
  • Survey (ALTA) — Confirm 0.5-acre lot size and no encroachments
  • Phase I ESA — Environmental review advisable even on 2017 construction
  • Rooftop / 3rd floor expansion — Confirm permit requirements and structural feasibility for 3,000 SF addition
  • Plug and Play lease expiration (July 2027) — 1,500 SF rollover risk within near-term horizon; confirm renewal plans
